perturbation.original_text: 1.32 "Indication" means the treatment of Dravet syndrome, Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, [***] during the Distribution Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.32 "Indication" means the treatment of Dravet syndrome, Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, [***] during the Distribution Term, and any disease.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Indication" limits it to the treatment of Dravet syndrome and Lennox-Gastaut syndrome. The modified definition expands it to “any disease”, creating a conflict. This contradiction occurs most acutely in Section 2.6, where the original "Indication" definition should restrict activities outside the specified conditions, but now it doesn't.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 No Activities Outside the Territory or Field. Distributor shall not, and shall cause its Affiliates and Sub-distributors not to, (a) actively seek customers for the Product outside the Territory or the Field, (b) establish or maintain a branch office, warehouse, or distribution facility for the Product outside the Territory, (c) engage in any advertising or promotional activities relating to the Product directed to customers outside the Territory or directed to any use outside the Field, or (d) solicit orders from any prospective purchaser with its principal place of business located outside the Territory. If Distributor receives any order from a prospective purchaser outside the Territory, Distributor shall not accept any such order but shall immediately refer that order to Zogenix.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of "Indication" was limited to specific conditions. The change expands the definition to include "any disease." This directly contradicts section 2.6, which restricts the distributor's activities to a specific "Field." By broadening the definition of "Indication" to include any disease, the restriction in section 2.6 regarding activities outside the field becomes unenforceable, as the "Field" is now effectively unlimited. This creates a clear and direct conflict, leading to uncertainty and a potential inability to enforce the original intent of the restriction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Grant of Rights to Distributor. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Zogenix hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor accepts appointment, as the exclusive distributor (even as to Zogenix) of the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term, and grants to Distributor the exclusive rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of (while Distributor is the MAH Party), package, promote, market, offer for sale, sell, import and distribute the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Grant of Rights to Distributor. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Zogenix hereby appoints Distributor, and Distributor accepts appointment, as the non-exclusive distributor of the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term, and grants to Distributor the rights to maintain Regulatory Approval of (while Distributor is the MAH Party), package, promote, market, offer for sale, sell, import and distribute the Product in the Field in the Territory during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants the distributor *exclusive* rights. The modified text changes the distributor to a *non-exclusive* distributor. This directly contradicts the intention of exclusivity established. This in-text contradiction will affect 5.1(a), since distributor no longer have exclusive rights to commercialize the product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Commercialization of the Product. (a) Distributor Responsibilities. Distributor shall have the exclusive right to market, promote, sell, offer for sale, import, package and otherwise Commercialize the Product in the Field in the Territory, at its sole cost and expense, in accordance with Applicable Laws and the Commercialization Plan and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Without limiting the foregoing, Distributor will have the exclusive right and responsibility in the Field in the Territory for the following: (i) designing the Commercialization strategy and tactics for the Product, subject to JSC approval of the Commercialization Plan; (ii) undertaking all promotional activities for the Product; (iii) establishing and implementing post-marketing surveillance studies for the Product in the Territory as required or recommended by a Regulatory Authority; (iv) receiving, accepting and filling orders for the Product from customers; (v) warehousing and distributing the Product to customers; (vi) controlling invoicing, order processing and collection of accounts receivable for sales of the Product; (vii) recording sales of the Product in the Territory in its books of account for sales; and (viii) providing results of sales of the Product in the Territory for purposes of periodic safety reports and exposure estimates. Distributor shall provide updates regularly to the JSC relating to Commercialization activities for the Product in the Field in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2.1 grants the distributor *exclusive* rights, while the changed text grants the distributor *non-exclusive* rights. This directly contradicts Section 5.1(a), which states that 'Distributor shall have the exclusive right to market, promote, sell, offer for sale, import, package and otherwise Commercialize the Product in the Field in the Territory'. The change from exclusive to non-exclusive directly contradicts and undermines the distributor's exclusive rights as outlined in Section 5.1(a).

perturbation.original_text: 10.5 Limitation of Liability. NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE ENTITLED TO RECOVER FROM THE OTHER PARTY ANY SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT; provided however, that this Section 10.5 shall not be construed to limit (a) either Party's right to special, incidental or consequential damages for the other Party's breach of Article 8 or (b) either Party's indemnification rights or obligations under Article 11.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.5 Limitation of Liability. NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE ENTITLED TO RECOVER FROM THE OTHER PARTY ANY SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT; provided however, that this Section 10.5 shall be construed to limit (a) either Party's right to special, incidental or consequential damages for the other Party's breach of Article 8 or (b) either Party's indemnification rights or obligations under Article 11.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says the Section 10.5 should *not* be construed to limit the right to special damages under the exceptions (a) and (b). The change here is changing "shall not" to "shall" -- meaning that it is *to be* construed that it limits these exceptions. This text will cause an in-text contradiction within Article 11, since 11.1 and 11.2 reffers the Third Party Claims and is contradicted directly by this change that doesn't allow the recovery from the other party of any Losses that arise.
perturbation.location: Article 10. Representations, Warranties and Covenants; Limitation of Liability - 10.5
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Indemnification of Zogenix. Distributor shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Zogenix and its Affiliates and their respective directors, officers, employees and agents (the "Zogenix Indemnitees"), from and against any and all losses, liabilities, damages, penalties, fines, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and other expenses of litigation) ("Losses") incurred by any Zogenix Indemnitee resulting from any claims, actions, suits or proceedings brought by a Third Party ("Third Party Claims") to the extent arising from, or occurring as a result of: (a) the registration, use, handling, storage, import, offer for sale, sale or other disposition of the Product in the Territory by or on behalf of Distributor or its Affiliates; (b) the negligence or willful misconduct of any Distributor Indemnitees in connection with Distributor's performance of its obligations or exercise of its rights under this Agreement; or (c) any breach of any representations, warranties or covenants of Distributor in this Agreement, except to the extent such Third Party Claims fall within the scope of the indemnification obligations of Zogenix set forth in Section 11.2 or arise out of the breach by Zogenix of any of the terms of the Supply Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10.5 now states that the limitation of liability *shall* be construed to limit the right to special damages for breach of Article 8 and indemnification rights under Article 11. This directly contradicts Article 11.1, which requires the Distributor to indemnify Zogenix for Losses arising from Third Party Claims. By limiting the ability to recover special, incidental, or consequential damages as it relates to Article 11, Section 10.5 now prevents Zogenix from being fully indemnified for Losses as defined in and required by Section 11.1, creating a direct conflict and thus an in-text contradiction. The original text, by saying that section 10.5 "shall not" limit the exceptions, was allowing Zogenix to be fully indemnified. The change makes it so that Zogenix cannot be fully indemnified, since section 10.5 is *now* limiting this right.
